comment: Hand-curated Iceland attractions. months = Jan-first 12 scores: 0 closed/inaccessible · 1 possible-but-marginal · 2 good · 3 peak. Edit freely — build-curated.mjs validates ids/enums/coords on every push. photo:null is fine (wiki enrichment fills thumbnails).

attractions.name: Hallgrímskirkja
attractions.description: Reykjavík's rocket-shaped landmark church, inspired by basalt columns. The tower elevator gives the best view over the city's colorful rooftops.

attractions.name: Harpa Concert Hall
attractions.description: Glittering honeycomb-glass concert hall on the old harbour, free to wander. The facade plays with light year-round — especially at winter dusk.

attractions.name: Sun Voyager
attractions.description: Steel dream-boat sculpture on the seafront with Esja mountain across the bay. A two-minute stop that earns its photo.

attractions.name: Perlan — Wonders of Iceland
attractions.description: Nature museum in the hot-water tanks on Öskjuhlíð hill: a real indoor ice cave, northern-lights planetarium show, and a 360° observation deck.

attractions.name: National Museum of Iceland
attractions.description: Iceland's story from Viking settlement to today, anchored by the Valþjófsstaður church door. The best single indoor primer before touring the country.

attractions.name: Reykjavík Old Harbour
attractions.description: Working harbour turned lively quarter — whale-watching departures, fish restaurants, and the Grandi food-and-design strip.

attractions.name: Sky Lagoon
attractions.description: Oceanfront infinity-edge geothermal lagoon 10 minutes from downtown, with a seven-step bathing ritual. Winter soaks can come with aurora overhead.

attractions.name: Laugardalslaug Pool
attractions.description: Reykjavík's biggest public pool complex — hot pots, steam, slides, sea-water tub. The cheap, local way to do Icelandic bathing culture.

attractions.name: Grótta Lighthouse
attractions.description: Seltjarnarnes headland with a tidal-island lighthouse — the capital's classic aurora-watching spot thanks to low light pollution. Check tides; closed May–mid-July for nesting birds.
attractions.seasonNote: Island closed ~May 1–Jul 15 (bird nesting); aurora season Sep–Apr

attractions.name: Blue Lagoon
attractions.description: The milky-blue silica lagoon in a black lava field — Iceland's most famous bath. Pre-booking essential; occasionally closes during Reykjanes eruptions.

attractions.name: Bridge Between Continents
attractions.description: Footbridge over a sandy rift where the North American and Eurasian plates pull apart. A quick, fun geology stop near Keflavík.

attractions.name: Gunnuhver Hot Springs
attractions.description: Iceland's largest mud pool hisses and steams at the tip of Reykjanes, named for a ghost said to be trapped in the spring. Boardwalk viewing only.

attractions.name: Reykjanesviti & Valahnúkamöl
attractions.description: Iceland's oldest lighthouse above a wave-battered cliff coast, with the sea stack Karl offshore. Dramatic in any weather — brutal in a winter storm.

attractions.name: Brimketill
attractions.description: A natural lava rock pool carved by surf at the shoreline — legend says a troll bathed here. Viewing platform; never climb down in swell.

attractions.name: Seltún Geothermal Area
attractions.description: Boardwalks through boiling mud pots and sulfur-painted hillsides at Krýsuvík — the most colorful geothermal field within an hour of Reykjavík.

attractions.name: Kleifarvatn
attractions.description: Deep, moody rift lake framed by black sand shores and steep scree — an eerie, often-empty stop on the Krýsuvík loop.

attractions.name: Fagradalsfjall Eruption Site
attractions.description: Hike over the 2021–2023 lava fields of the Reykjanes fires — still-steaming basalt and fresh craters. Trails close during new unrest; check safetravel.is first.

attractions.name: Þingvellir National Park
attractions.description: UNESCO site where the Viking parliament met from 930 AD, set in the rift valley between two tectonic plates. Walk Almannagjá gorge; snorkel Silfra fissure.

attractions.name: Geysir & Strokkur
attractions.description: The hot-spring field that named all geysers. Great Geysir sleeps, but Strokkur erupts 15–30 m every few minutes — you will not wait long.

attractions.name: Gullfoss
attractions.description: The 'Golden Falls' — Hvítá river thundering down a two-tier, 32 m staircase into a canyon. Winter dresses it in ice; summer adds rainbows.

attractions.name: Kerið Crater
attractions.description: A 3,000-year-old volcanic crater with red slopes and an opal-green lake. Rim walk takes 15 minutes; steps lead down to the water.

attractions.name: Brúarfoss
attractions.description: The 'bluest waterfall in Iceland' — glacial meltwater churning electric blue through a small cascade. Now reachable by a short walk from a paid car park.

attractions.name: Secret Lagoon
attractions.description: Iceland's oldest swimming pool (1891) at Flúðir — a simple, steamy natural pool with a tiny geyser bubbling beside it. The low-key Blue Lagoon alternative.

attractions.name: Friðheimar Tomato Farm
attractions.description: Lunch inside a geothermal greenhouse among the tomato vines — famous tomato soup with fresh bread. Book ahead; also show horses in summer.

attractions.name: Hjálparfoss
attractions.description: Twin falls braiding through basalt into one pool in Þjórsárdalur valley — a quiet, five-minute-from-the-car gem most tours skip.

attractions.name: Seljalandsfoss
attractions.description: The waterfall you can walk behind — a 60 m ribbon off the old sea cliffs. Bring a rain shell; the path behind closes when icy.
attractions.seasonNote: Behind-the-falls path usually closed in winter (ice)

attractions.name: Gljúfrabúi
attractions.description: Seljalandsfoss's hidden neighbor — wade a few steps up a stream into a mossy slot canyon where the fall drops through a skylight. Waterproof boots help.

attractions.name: Seljavallalaug
attractions.description: A 1923 concrete pool tucked into a green mountain valley, fed by a warm spring — reached by a 20-minute walk. Rustic: unheated changing shed, algae-soft walls.

attractions.name: Skógafoss
attractions.description: A perfect 60 m curtain of water you can walk right up to — expect double rainbows on sunny days. Stairs climb to the rim and the Fimmvörðuháls trail.

attractions.name: Kvernufoss
attractions.description: Skógafoss's quiet cousin one gully east — a 30 m fall you can also walk behind, usually shared with only a handful of people.

attractions.name: Skógar Museum
attractions.description: Turf houses, a fishing boat, and 18,000 artifacts of how Icelanders actually survived here — one of the country's best folk museums.

attractions.name: LAVA Centre
attractions.description: Interactive volcano and earthquake exhibition at Hvolsvöllur with live monitoring feeds — excellent context before driving under Eyjafjallajökull.

attractions.name: Sólheimajökull
attractions.description: An easily-reached outlet glacier tongue streaked with ash — a 15-minute walk from the car park views it; going ONTO the ice requires a guided walk.
attractions.feeNote: Parking fee; glacier walks guided only

attractions.name: Sólheimasandur Plane Wreck
attractions.description: The 1973 US Navy DC-3 wreck on an empty black-sand flat. It's a flat but exposed 4 km walk each way (or paid shuttle) — miserable in bad weather.

attractions.name: Dyrhólaey
attractions.description: A huge sea arch and lighthouse promontory with black beaches running to the horizon both ways — and puffins burrowing all over it in summer.
attractions.seasonNote: Partial closures ~May 1–Jun 25 for nesting; upper road is steep

attractions.name: Reynisfjara Black Beach
attractions.description: Basalt-column cliffs, roaring surf, and the Reynisdrangar sea stacks. Beautiful and genuinely dangerous — sneaker waves kill; obey the light-signal warnings and never turn your back on the sea.

attractions.name: Vík í Mýrdal
attractions.description: Iceland's southernmost village under the Reynisdrangar stacks — red-roofed church viewpoint, wool outlet, and the natural overnight base for the south coast.

attractions.name: Fjaðrárgljúfur
attractions.description: A 100 m deep, serpentine moss-walled canyon with a rim path and viewing platforms. The short gravel access road can close in wet/winter conditions.

attractions.name: Kirkjubæjarklaustur
attractions.description: Small service town between Vík and Skaftafell — fuel, food, and the 'church floor' of naturally tiled basalt just outside it. Handy overnight link.

attractions.name: Heimaey (Westman Islands)
attractions.description: Ferry-hop island town half-buried by the 1973 eruption — climb the still-warm Eldfell cone, visit the excellent Eldheimar museum, and meet Europe's biggest puffin colony in summer.

attractions.name: Eldfell
attractions.description: The red cinder cone born overnight in 1973 above Heimaey's rooftops. An easy 30–40 minute climb; the ground is still warm in places.

attractions.name: Skaftafell
attractions.description: Vatnajökull National Park's hiking hub between glacier tongues — trails from 30 minutes to all day, plus guided ice walks. Start at the visitor centre.

attractions.name: Svartifoss
attractions.description: The 'Black Falls' pouring over a amphitheater of hanging basalt organ pipes — a 1.8 km uphill walk from Skaftafell that inspired Hallgrímskirkja's design.

attractions.name: Svínafellsjökull
attractions.description: A crevasse-wrinkled glacier tongue you can view from a short lakeside path — filmed for Interstellar and Game of Thrones. Rough access track.

attractions.name: Múlagljúfur Canyon
attractions.description: A steep green canyon with waterfalls threading down both walls and Öræfajökull looming behind — a 2.5 km unmarked-but-clear trail keeps crowds thin.

attractions.name: Fjallsárlón
attractions.description: Jökulsárlón's smaller, quieter sibling — icebergs calving close to shore beneath a steep glacier face. Zodiac boat tours in summer.

attractions.name: Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon
attractions.description: Blue icebergs drifting from Breiðamerkurjökull out to sea, often with seals weaving between them. Amphibian and zodiac tours May–Oct; stunning in every season.

attractions.name: Diamond Beach
attractions.description: Lagoon icebergs washed onto black sand and polished into glassy 'diamonds' — best light at low sun. Right across the bridge from Jökulsárlón.

attractions.name: Höfn
attractions.description: Harbor town famous for langoustine (humar) with Vatnajökull filling the horizon — the natural overnight stop between the lagoons and the East Fjords.

attractions.name: Vestrahorn (Stokksnes)
attractions.description: Spiky 454 m batholith rising straight from a black-sand tidal flat — photographers' favorite mountain in Iceland. Private access road with a small fee.

attractions.name: Eggin í Gleðivík
attractions.description: 34 giant granite eggs along Djúpivogur's harbor road, one per local nesting bird — a quirky art walk in a slow-food fishing village.

attractions.name: Petra's Stone Collection
attractions.description: One woman's lifetime of jasper, agate and zeolites filling a garden in Stöðvarfjörður — the East Fjords' most beloved oddball stop.

attractions.name: Seyðisfjörður
attractions.description: Artsy fjord village at the foot of a switchback mountain pass — the rainbow street to the blue church, cafes, and the Smyril Line ferry. The pass is a real winter drive.

attractions.name: Hengifoss
attractions.description: Iceland's third-highest fall (128 m) dropping through cliff bands striped red with ancient clay — a 2.5 km climb passing basalt-columned Litlanesfoss.

attractions.name: Stuðlagil Canyon
attractions.description: Towering basalt colonnades over a teal glacial river, revealed when a dam lowered the water. East-bank hike (5 km round) gets the famous view.

attractions.name: Vök Baths
attractions.description: Floating geothermal pools out on lake Urriðavatn near Egilsstaðir — swim-up tea bar, cold-plunge into the lake, low-key East Iceland vibes.

attractions.name: Borgarfjörður Eystri Puffins
attractions.description: Hafnarhólmi harbor islet with boardwalks through ~10,000 puffin burrows at arm's length — the easiest, closest puffin viewing in Iceland (mid-April to early August).

attractions.name: Dettifoss
attractions.description: Europe's most powerful waterfall — 100 m wide, gray with glacial sediment, throwing spray you can see from a kilometer away. West-side road 862 is paved.

attractions.name: Ásbyrgi Canyon
attractions.description: A horseshoe canyon 3.5 km long with 100 m sheer walls around a birch forest and echoing pond — legend calls it Sleipnir's hoofprint.

attractions.name: Húsavík Whale Watching
attractions.description: Europe's whale-watching capital — humpbacks near-guaranteed in summer on Skjálfandi bay, plus the Whale Museum and the wooden church on the harbor.

attractions.name: GeoSea Baths
attractions.description: Hot SEAWATER infinity pools on Húsavík's cliff edge — watch whale spouts from the water. Small, book ahead in summer.

attractions.name: Goðafoss
attractions.description: The 'Waterfall of the Gods,' where Iceland's lawspeaker reportedly threw his pagan idols on converting in 1000 AD — a wide, elegant horseshoe right off the Ring Road.

attractions.name: Lake Mývatn
attractions.description: Shallow volcanic lake dotted with pseudocraters and absurdly rich birdlife (every European duck species nests here). The name means 'midge lake' — bring a head net in July.

attractions.name: Dimmuborgir
attractions.description: The 'Dark Fortresses' — a labyrinth of collapsed lava pillars and arches with easy loop trails. Home of the Yule Lads in Icelandic folklore.

attractions.name: Grjótagjá
attractions.description: A small rift cave holding a steaming blue pool (that Game of Thrones scene). Bathing is prohibited — the water runs unpredictably hot — but the peek inside takes minutes.

attractions.name: Hverir Geothermal Area
attractions.description: A Mars-orange plain of roaring steam vents and burping mud cauldrons under Námafjall ridge. The sulfur smell is unforgettable; the colors even more so.

attractions.name: Mývatn Nature Baths
attractions.description: The north's answer to the Blue Lagoon — milky alkaline water overlooking the lake plain, at half the crowds. Magic at midnight sun or under winter aurora.

attractions.name: Krafla & Víti Crater
attractions.description: Turquoise crater lake ('Hell') at the Krafla volcano, ringed by the steaming lava of the 1975–84 Krafla Fires. Short rim walk; road closed by snow much of winter.

attractions.name: Aldeyjarfoss
attractions.description: A white torrent punching through a black basalt-column bowl at the edge of the highlands — reached by a rough gravel spur from the Sprengisandur road.

attractions.name: Akureyri
attractions.description: The 'capital of the north' — botanical garden at 65°N, heart-shaped traffic lights, good food street (Hafnarstræti), and a winter ski hill above the fjord.

attractions.name: Forest Lagoon
attractions.description: Infinity pools set INSIDE a birch forest across the fjord from Akureyri — a rare trees-and-steam combo in a mostly treeless country. Swim-up bar, sauna, cold plunge.

attractions.name: Siglufjörður
attractions.description: Once the herring capital of the Atlantic, now a photogenic fjord town with the award-winning Herring Era Museum and a craft brewery — reached through single-lane tunnels.

attractions.name: Hofsós Pool
attractions.description: A simple town pool with an architectural trick: the edge visually merges with Skagafjörður below, like swimming into the fjord. Best cheap infinity pool in Iceland.

attractions.name: Glaumbær Turf Farm
attractions.description: The best-preserved turf farmhouse complex in Iceland — a warren of grass-roofed rooms showing how families lived into the 1940s.

attractions.name: Hvítserkur
attractions.description: A 15 m basalt sea stack shaped like a drinking dragon (or troll caught by sunrise), guano-painted white — with seals hauling out on the sandbars nearby.

attractions.name: Kolugljúfur
attractions.description: A compact gorge where the Víðidalsá river stacks several waterfalls between mossy walls — a two-minute walk from a tiny bridge, and usually empty.

attractions.name: Grímsey (Arctic Circle)
attractions.description: The only piece of Iceland on the Arctic Circle — ferry or flight to a 100-person island of puffin cliffs and the 'Orbis et Globus' sphere that moves with the circle each year.
attractions.feeNote: Ferry from Dalvík / flight from Akureyri

attractions.name: Dynjandi
attractions.description: The Westfjords' crown — a 100 m bridal-veil staircase widening as it falls, with six smaller falls on the walk up. Worth the long drive on its own.
attractions.seasonNote: Mountain roads to it often closed Nov–Apr

attractions.name: Látrabjarg
attractions.description: Europe's westernmost point: 14 km of cliffs up to 441 m stacked with millions of seabirds. The puffins at the top edge are famously unbothered by people — stay back from the crumbly rim.

attractions.name: Rauðisandur
attractions.description: Ten kilometers of red-gold sand (crushed scallop shells) shifting color with the light, reached by a hairpin gravel descent. Seals in the lagoon; a tiny black church at Saurbær.

attractions.name: Ísafjörður
attractions.description: The Westfjords' hub, squeezed onto a sandspit under 700 m walls — old timber merchant houses, great bakeries and kayaking, gateway to Hornstrandir wilderness.

attractions.name: Museum of Icelandic Sorcery & Witchcraft
attractions.description: Hólmavík's deadpan little museum of 17th-century witch burnings, rune staves — and the infamous necropants. Strange, dark, memorable.

attractions.name: Glymur
attractions.description: Iceland's second-highest waterfall (198 m) hidden in Hvalfjörður — a proper 3–4 hour loop hike with a cave, a log river crossing (summer only) and vertiginous canyon views.
attractions.seasonNote: River log removed off-season — full loop summer only

attractions.name: Deildartunguhver
attractions.description: Europe's most powerful hot spring — 180 L/s of boiling water blasting from a red rock face, piped off to heat towns 60 km away. Krauma baths sit beside it.

attractions.name: Krauma Baths
attractions.description: Sleek small baths mixing Deildartunguhver's boiling water with glacial melt — five pools, cold plunge, fire-lit relaxation room. Rarely crowded.

attractions.name: Reykholt & Snorrastofa
attractions.description: Home of Snorri Sturluson, who wrote down the Norse myths before being murdered here in 1241 — his (probable) geothermal bathing pool survives.

attractions.name: Hraunfossar & Barnafoss
attractions.description: A kilometer of spring water seeping OUT of a lava field into the turquoise Hvítá — utterly unlike a normal waterfall — with the churning chute of Barnafoss a short walk upstream.

attractions.name: Víðgelmir Lava Cave
attractions.description: Iceland's largest lava tube — 1.6 km of ice formations and mineral colors on an easy boardwalk, guided year-round from Fljótstunga farm.

attractions.name: Into the Glacier (Langjökull)
attractions.description: A man-made ice tunnel bored 30 m deep into Langjökull's blue heart, reached by monster truck from Húsafell — the year-round way to stand inside a glacier.

attractions.name: Grábrók Crater
attractions.description: A perfectly-formed 170 m scoria cone right beside the Ring Road with a stairway to the rim — a 30-minute leg-stretch with a big payoff view over Norðurárdalur.

attractions.name: Ytri-Tunga Seal Beach
attractions.description: A golden (not black!) beach where harbor seals haul out on the rocks year-round — best at low tide. Keep 50 m distance and they'll stay put.

attractions.name: Búðakirkja (Black Church)
attractions.description: A lone black-tarred church from 1847 standing in the Búðahraun lava field between mountains and sea — minimalist Iceland in one frame.

attractions.name: Rauðfeldsgjá
attractions.description: A narrow cleft splitting the mountainside — squeeze in along the stream to a mossy, echoing chamber. Named for a boy pushed in by his uncle in the sagas.

attractions.name: Arnarstapi Cliffs
attractions.description: Basalt arches (Gatklettur), blowholes and kittiwake colonies along the cliff path to Hellnar — the classic easy coastal walk of Snæfellsnes, under Stapafell's pyramid.

attractions.name: Hellnar
attractions.description: Tiny former fishing village at the end of the Arnarstapi cliff walk — the cave Baðstofa, a cliff-top cafe, and huge Atlantic views.

attractions.name: Lóndrangar
attractions.description: Two volcanic plugs (75 m and 61 m) rising like a cathedral from the surf, with fulmars wheeling around them — a five-minute walk from the viewpoint car park.

attractions.name: Vatnshellir Cave
attractions.description: An 8,000-year-old lava tube descending 35 m on spiral stairs into total darkness — 45-minute guided tours run year-round; Jules Verne started his journey to the center of the Earth nearby.

attractions.name: Djúpalónssandur
attractions.description: Black pearl-pebble beach strewn with the rusted wreck of a 1948 British trawler and the four legendary 'lifting stones' fishermen used to prove their strength. Do not swim — the surf is lethal.

attractions.name: Snæfellsjökull
attractions.description: The glacier-capped stratovolcano crowning the peninsula — Jules Verne's gateway to the center of the Earth. Viewable from everywhere; summiting is a guided spring/summer trip.

attractions.name: Kirkjufell & Kirkjufellsfoss
attractions.description: The most-photographed mountain in Iceland — the arrowhead peak by Grundarfjörður, framed behind its little triple waterfall. Iconic under aurora; climbing the peak itself is dangerous.

attractions.name: Stykkishólmur
attractions.description: Colorful harbor town facing Breiðafjörður's 3,000 islands — Norwegian-era wooden houses, the basalt library of water, ferry to Flatey island, and famous mussels.

attractions.name: Landmannalaugar
attractions.description: Rhyolite mountains in ochre, pink and green around a natural hot river you soak in after hiking — the highlands' crown jewel and the Laugavegur trailhead. F-roads only.
attractions.seasonNote: F208/F224 open ~late Jun–Sep; winter access by super-jeep tour only

attractions.name: Þórsmörk
attractions.description: 'Thor's valley' — a green birch oasis walled by three glaciers, with canyon hikes (Stakkholtsgjá) and big-view ridges. The F249 approach fords serious rivers: bus or super-jeep, not your rental.

attractions.name: Háifoss & Granni
attractions.description: Iceland's fourth-highest fall (122 m) plunging beside its twin Granni into a huge canyon at the edge of the highlands — a rough 4x4 gravel track or a 2-hour walk from below.

attractions.name: Gjáin
attractions.description: A hidden pocket valley of waterfalls, clear streams, columns and elf-story greenery in barren Þjórsárdalur — like a terrarium dropped into the moonscape.

attractions.name: Sigöldugljúfur
attractions.description: The 'Valley of Tears' — dozens of spring-fed ribbons weeping down a turquoise canyon near the Landmannalaugar road. A short walk from a rough track; drone-famous, still quiet.

attractions.name: Kerlingarfjöll — Hveradalir
attractions.description: Steam pouring from ochre-red ridges between mini-glaciers at Hveradalir — the most otherworldly geothermal valley in Iceland. Via the Kjölur road (35) plus a mountain spur.
attractions.seasonNote: Open ~late Jun–Sep; winter snowcat tours exist

attractions.name: Hveravellir
attractions.description: A steaming oasis halfway across the Kjölur highland route — fumaroles, a soakable hot pool, and outlaw Fjalla-Eyvindur's 18th-century hideout.

attractions.name: Askja & Víti
attractions.description: A vast caldera in the Dyngjufjöll mountains holding deep-blue Öskjuvatn and the milky, swimmable crater Víti. Apollo astronauts trained here. Serious F-road expedition with river fords.
attractions.seasonNote: F88/F905/F910 open ~Jul–Sep; guided day tours from Mývatn
